title: Is there mehtod to reduce the time of C++ file generation or debug which module causes the long compile time?
body:
I am trying verilator on two designs. Both of them hit the long compile time issue.

One of them is about 40MGates. (reported by emulator synthesis tool)

With the basic option (no --trace --debug), it took about 20 hours to generate C++ file.

```shell
verilator --output-split 10000 --exe -Wno-WIDTH -Wno-fatal -Wno-MODDUP  -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD -Wno-BLKANDBBLK -Mdir obj_dir -cc -02 --top-module chip_tb -j 10 --timescale-override 1ps/1ps -sv -f dut.flist chip_tb.sv -f ./model.f -f ./define.flist sim_main.cpp
```

I want to check if any module has complex structure, which makes verilator slow.
but I don't know if any method is provided except enabling --debug option to output large log file.
With log file, actually it's still hard to figure out the useful information.
